name: stronghold-lookout
purpose: Operator tooling repo for Stronghold, now spanning both the Go CLI runtime and a React/TypeScript web cockpit for same-origin auth, NATS transport, and estate visibility.
status: active CLI plus web cockpit repo with principal-aware session state, production-shaped browser shell, blank product dashboard, tucked session/connection menu, reusable resource-interface contract shell, read-first authority/edge surfaces, account authentication-method management, controlled authority mutations with first-slice Level 3 command-auth signing, browser-local Ed25519 command-signing key lifecycle, key lifecycle audit readback, grant-backed NATS WebSocket connection flow, and Foundation Verification Gate truth panels
repo_role_in_system: Operator control surface and config tooling for the Stronghold estate
depends_on: Stronghold principal/key authority and envelope trust posture

service_boundaries.cli_runtime: Go/Cobra CLI for login, whoami, config get/set/push, message capture, resource-interface contract inspection, and operator workflows over NATS, including NATS_CREDS_FILE-based native JWT credentials for live config pushes.
service_boundaries.web_shell: React/Vite browser cockpit with shell layout, static module registry, blank product Dashboard landing page, grouped left navigation that separates Home, Manage, Security, Operations, and Debug surfaces, upper-right session/NATS operator menu, reusable resource-interface contract route, and read-first Debug Cockpit, Sentry, Aegis, and Authority operator surfaces.
service_boundaries.session_client: Same-origin browser session boundary that opens Drawbridge auth, listens for callback completion, refreshes /_/auth/session, calls same-origin logout, normalizes separate root and active principal metadata, and discovers the Sentry browser transport grant path without storing credentials.
service_boundaries.authority_read_client: Typed authority read client that falls back to same-origin HTTP under /_/authority/read, but prefers browser NATS request/reply with the short-lived Sentry grant token once the /_/nats rail is connected. It includes account authentication-method posture, context hierarchy posture, badge archive state, direct/subtree grant provenance, service definition/context-binding readback, key posture, and authority audit-event readback.
service_boundaries.authority_mutation_client: Typed same-origin HTTP client for controlled Sentry authority mutations under /_/authority/mutate with idempotency keys and operator confirmation states, including account authentication-method link/status/revoke, context identity enrollment, person subject create/invite, signed context create/update, context-scoped badge-definition create/update/archive, scoped principal-badge grant, context-service provision, and principal-key lifecycle controls. Context create/update, badge grant/revoke, context-service provision, and principal key revoke/rotate attach Level 3 command-auth signatures when a browser signer is ready.
service_boundaries.nats_browser_transport: NATS over WebSocket browser rail used by the cockpit through /_/nats only when session bootstrap reports transport.ready=true and Sentry confirms transport_ready=true while returning a delegated-principal native NATS credential through the same-origin grant path. The grant token is kept in memory for authorized NATS authority reads; grant posture metadata exposes source, expiry, permission counts, and denial state without persisting secrets; browser-side probes can verify active-principal and signed delegated service/agent credential allow/deny behavior, including unsigned/replay/forged-header/unowned-principal negative checks; and the static bootstrap token is never used as a browser credential.
service_boundaries.browser_command_signing: Level 3 Lookout Web signer lifecycle for active-principal command authorship. Generates non-exportable native WebCrypto Ed25519 keys, stores the private CryptoKey in IndexedDB, registers only rawurl-base64 public key material with Sentry, selects matching active principal key records, and signs command payloads with active identity plus active principal binding before attaching X-Stronghold-Command-Auth.
service_boundaries.lookout_truth_panels: Debug Cockpit and authority surfaces expose runtime truth indicators for transport grant source, credential expiry, permission-envelope shape, service permission-lane shape, in-tab NATS allow/deny probe results, signed delegated transport drill results, negative transport drill results, last observed denied rail/read/mutation action, active authority read rail, command-signer custody boundaries, and memory-only replay posture.
service_boundaries.resource_interface_contract: LRI-01/LRI-02/LRI-03/LRI-04 reusable List/Create/Detail/Edit/Lifecycle shell and static contract route at /debug/resource-interface. It defines resource record summaries, selected-record detail posture, relationship cards, raw-id inspectability, empty/error states, confirmation-backed lifecycle action placement, evidence feedback, quick filtering, basic sorting, and visible-column toggles over loaded records without converting authority resources or pretending to provide backend/global search yet.
service_boundaries.config_templates: Tracked JSON files remain examples/templates rather than live estate truth.
service_boundaries.context_manager_read_surface: CAM-01 plus LRI-05 product-facing Contexts manager read surface. The context route now uses the shared resource-interface shell to compose context hierarchy posture, enrolled identity counts, badge counts, direct/inherited grant counts, relationship cards, raw IDs, and local list controls from existing authority reads while keeping mutations and debug truth panels separate.
service_boundaries.context_manager_mutation_surface: CAM-02 product-facing Contexts manager mutation surface. Context create/update submissions now follow the same Level 3 browser command-signing posture as backend Sentry enforcement instead of relying on session authorization alone.
service_boundaries.badge_manager_lifecycle_surface: CAM-03 plus LRI-06 product-facing Badges manager surface. The badge route now uses the shared resource-interface shell to compose context read posture with badge definitions, render context names/scope next to badge records, expose archive posture, relationship cards, raw IDs, and local list controls, while keeping create/update/archive submissions in the controlled mutation panel.
service_boundaries.context_identity_enrollment_surface: CAM-04 plus LRI-07 product-facing Identities manager surface. The identity route now uses the shared resource-interface shell to compose accounts, contexts, identities, paired principal posture, relationship cards, raw IDs, and local list controls so operators can see account/context labels beside each identity record before using the controlled mutation panel for enrollment or subject creation.
service_boundaries.person_subject_creation_surface: CAM-06 person subject creation surface. The Identities mutation form can switch from existing-account enrollment to person create/invite, capture email, interactive-login posture, and optional personal-context provisioning, then submit the Sentry subject.create contract.
service_boundaries.personal_context_provisioning_option: CAM-08 personal context option exposed in the Identities manager. Operators can explicitly choose whether a person subject gets a separate personal context; the UI does not imply every subject or every shared-context identity receives one.
service_boundaries.agent_service_key_bootstrap_surface: CAM-07 agent/service bootstrap controls. The Identities mutation form can create agent/service subjects with token-key posture, key_id, Ed25519 public_key, and service_key while making clear that Sentry receives only public key material.
service_boundaries.identity_facing_grant_manager: CAM-09 plus LRI-08 identity-facing badge grant manager. The Grants route now uses the shared resource-interface shell to show badge, principal, identity, scope_mode, inherited/effective context posture, revoke state, relationship cards, raw IDs, and local list controls. The Grants mutation form still selects identities and submits their paired principal_id through the existing principal_badge_grants compatibility bridge, with direct/subtree scope_mode preserved for backend enforcement.
service_boundaries.service_agent_key_resource_surfaces: LRI-09 resource-interface conversion for service, agent/principal, and key posture. Services show shared definitions, context bindings, and permission lanes; Principals show human/service/agent/ephemeral execution identities with provenance separated from grants; Keys show principal key records, issuer binding posture, expiry, and revocation state without exposing private material.
service_boundaries.resource_interface_evidence: LRI-10 CLI parity and evidence bundle. The CLI exposes offline `lookout resources contract` as a JSON doctrine contract for agent alignment, and web/RESOURCE_INTERFACE_EVIDENCE.md records converted surfaces, standard verbs, boundaries, validation commands, and known Vite chunk warning.

critical_invariants: Tracked JSON files are templates/examples, not live secret-bearing runtime configs.
Live edge config should come from generated Stronghold runtime artifacts, then be pushed via Lookout.
The web cockpit assumes same-origin auth under /_/auth and NATS transport under /_/nats.
The browser shell must not depend on reading the HttpOnly auth cookie directly; it consumes bootstrap/session payloads instead.
The browser shell must not receive reusable transport credentials from session bootstrap payloads.
The browser shell may request a short-lived scoped grant from the Sentry route advertised by session bootstrap, but must not store it in localStorage/sessionStorage.
Browser authority reads may use the in-memory Sentry transport grant over NATS request/reply after the rail connects; HTTP remains the compatibility fallback.
Account authentication methods prove account access only; Lookout must keep them separate from account ownership, identity posture, principal authority, and badge grants.
Browser NATS state must distinguish session/auth failure, runtime readiness disabled, credential minting failure, Sentry transport_ready denial, and WebSocket rail failure.
Verified command envelope enforcement for browser mutations depends on the explicit active-principal signing key lifecycle; NATS credentials must not be treated as command signatures.
Level 3 Lookout Web command signing requires native WebCrypto Ed25519; unsupported browsers should use Lookout Desktop instead of fallback algorithms or JavaScript crypto polyfills.
Lookout Web command signing stores non-exportable private CryptoKey material only in IndexedDB and registers only public Ed25519 key material with Sentry.

Lookout must attach command-auth signatures for context create/update, badge grant/revoke, context-service provision, and principal key revoke/rotate mutations; principal_key.register remains the browser signer bootstrap exception.
Lookout command signatures must include the active identity when available so one account cannot reuse a valid active-principal signature as another identity.
Static module registration is intentional and keeps the cockpit structure stable without runtime plugin machinery.

Lookout displays principal, badge, service, key, audit, transport, grant, signer, read-rail, and replay posture and can submit controlled Sentry mutations; it does not own principal key registration, envelope verification, badge truth, service binding truth, audit truth, replay truth, or authority issuance.
Read-first Sentry/Aegis pages should stay honest about backend gaps rather than implying mature browser-admin APIs exist.
known_risks: Authority cockpit reads prefer the browser NATS rail when connected and fall back to Sentry same-origin HTTP adapters; if the live edge config or transport grant path is stale, authorized sessions can still be denied.

The browser transport depends on the /_/nats rail accepting Sentry-minted delegated-principal NATS credentials; Lookout must not connect unless both session bootstrap and the Sentry grant response confirm readiness, and the static bootstrap NATS token must not be reused for browser, CLI, desktop, service, node, or agent actors.

Audit reads surface account authentication-method lifecycle, key lifecycle, context create/update, badge-definition lifecycle, principal badge grant/revoke, context service provisioning, and NATS transport credential evidence; remaining mutation families should be added deliberately as each authority surface hardens.

Lookout truth panels expose the current memory-only replay posture, observed in-tab denial state, and browser NATS allow/deny probe results, but they do not replace backend evidence bundles or broader adversarial smoke commands.
Template drift from generated runtime config is possible if bootstrap behavior changes and tracked examples are not refreshed.
Repo documentation still needs cleanup where older prose says no session bootstrap exists or says clients are unimplemented; the map reflects the current code, not those stale lines.
As Stronghold envelope enforcement becomes live, Lookout will need explicit UI/runtime posture for signed message authorship rather than only transport readiness.
